steps.title: Spot the clue about composing
steps.content: The passage says that while she is procrastinating and daydreaming, a vibe, a point, a phrase, a hook, a title, an image, a connection or an epiphany can emerge. She calls this activity composing even though no words appear right away. This shows that composing refers to the mental formation of ideas during her writing downtime.

steps.title: Apply the clue to the choices
steps.content: Choice B says she is engaging in an important part of her writing process, which directly matches the idea that daydreaming is part of composing. The other three choices either describe deception, source gathering, or being soothed, none of which capture the active idea‑forming process described. That matches choice B — B is correct.
